name: Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Document Search

parameters.text: =✅ Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Document Search — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06538-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: List Changed Docs (API)

### List Changed Docs (API)

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Remove Duplicate Documents

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Remove Duplicate Documents

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `docId`).

Next: Attach Chunk Metadata

### Attach Chunk Metadata

Custom JavaScript: Attach chunk-level metadata before embedding

Next: Insert into Vector Store

### Insert into Vector Store

Writes embedded chunks into the vector index.

Next: Count Indexed Chunks

🔑 Pinecone API key

### Prepare Documents

Wraps incoming items as documents (with metadata) for the vector store.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `document`

### Chunk with Overlap

Chunks documents (~1200 chars, 120 overlap) so retrieval hits stay focused.

Feeds **Prepare Documents** as `textSplitter`

### Document Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `embedding`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Count Indexed Chunks

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Document Search

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Document Search. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual document search is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → List Changed Docs (API) → Remove Duplicate Documents → Attach Chunk Metadata → Insert into Vector Store → Count Indexed Chunks → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating document search in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google AI API key; Pinecone API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your document search process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
